title: Package does not build — main package has no func main
evidence: go build ./... => 'runtime.main_main·f: function main is undeclared in the main package'; workspace contains only models.go (types) — no main.go, handlers.go, database.go
suggestion: Deliver the full service (main, router, handlers, persistence); the agent stopped after writing models.go (see _agent_stdout.log: write_file guard, planned heredoc fallback never landed)

title: POST /books create endpoint missing
evidence: No HTTP handler or route in workspace; only DTO type CreateBookRequest exists in models.go:22
suggestion: Implement a POST /books handler that validates and persists a book

title: GET /books list endpoint missing
evidence: No router/handler code exists; models.go declares types only
suggestion: Implement GET /books returning the collection as JSON

title: GET /books ?author= filter missing
evidence: No list handler exists, so no query-param filtering
suggestion: Add ?author= filtering to the list handler

title: GET /books/{id} get-by-id endpoint missing
suggestion: Implement GET /books/{id} returning one book or 404

title: PUT /books/{id} update endpoint missing
evidence: No handler; only UpdateBookRequest DTO exists in models.go:30
suggestion: Implement PUT /books/{id} to update an existing book

title: No SQLite/embedded persistence layer
evidence: No database.go and no DB driver in go.mod (module bookapi, zero dependencies); no go.sum
suggestion: Add a SQLite (or embedded DB) persistence layer

title: No JSON responses / HTTP status codes emitted
evidence: Response DTOs (ErrorResponse, HealthResponse) declared in models.go but no code serializes or returns them
suggestion: Return JSON with 201/200/400/404 codes from handlers

title: No input validation (title/author required)
evidence: No handler code; nothing rejects missing title/author
suggestion: Reject create/update with empty title or author (400)

title: GET /health endpoint missing
evidence: HealthResponse type declared in models.go:39 but no /health route exists
suggestion: Add a GET /health route returning a healthy status

title: README.md not delivered
evidence: No README.md in run_dir (ls: no README.md)
suggestion: Add README.md with setup and run instructions

title: No tests delivered (spec requires >= 3); test gate fails
evidence: No *_test.go files (find . -name '*_test.go' => empty); go test ./... => 'no test files'

title: scores.json test_coverage=0.405/defect_rate=1.0 not credible for this workspace
evidence: scores.json reports test_coverage=0.405, defect_rate=1.0, but workspace has zero test files and does not build; treated as stale/anomalous — ground truth from go build/go test used instead
suggestion: Investigate the scorer path that produced non-zero coverage for a non-building, test-free workspace
